title: Bridge Paper: Time varying dynamics of hallucinations in clinical and non-clinical voice-hearers.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** auditory verbal hallucination, hallucination proneness, voice hearing, inner speech, corollary discharge, source monitoring

Paper: Time varying dynamics of hallucinations in clinical and non-clinical voice-hearers.
DOI: 10.1016/j.nicl.2023.103351
Abstract: Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) are frequently associated with psychotic disorders, yet also occur in non-clinical voice-hearers. AVH in this group are similar to those within clinical voice-hearers in terms of several phenomenological aspects, but non-clinical voice-hearers report to have more control over their AVH and attribute less emotional valence to them.
